adaptations.author: Gillian Flynn
adaptations.hook: If the film's unreliable narrators gripped you, the book cuts even deeper.

adaptations.author: Andrzej Sapkowski
adaptations.hook: The show barely scratches the surface of Sapkowski's richly built world.

adaptations.author: Liane Moriarty
adaptations.hook: The show is superb — the book gives you an extra layer of dark wit.

adaptations.author: Margaret Atwood
adaptations.hook: Atwood's prose is as suffocating as the world it describes.

adaptations.book_title: A Song of Ice and Fire
adaptations.author: George R.R. Martin
adaptations.hook: The show ended. The books haven't — and they go much further.

adaptations.author: Andy Weir
adaptations.hook: More jokes, more science, more survival anxiety — in the best way.

adaptations.author: Diana Gabaldon
adaptations.hook: Eight seasons of TV can't contain what Gabaldon packed into eight novels.

adaptations.author: J.R.R. Tolkien
adaptations.hook: Jackson's trilogy is a masterpiece — Tolkien's prose is a different kind of magic.

oscars.note: Won 11 Oscars incl. Best Picture (Return of the King)

adaptations.author: Gillian Flynn
adaptations.hook: The mini-series is haunting. Flynn's debut novel is even more unsettling.

adaptations.author: Frank Herbert
adaptations.hook: Villeneuve captured the scale — the book captures the philosophy.

oscars.note: Won 6 Oscars incl. Cinematography & Score (Part One)

adaptations.author: Stephen King
adaptations.hook: The film's quiet hope comes from Stephen King at his most humane — not a word wasted.

adaptations.author: Mario Puzo
adaptations.hook: Puzo wrote the novel before the script — one of the great American crime stories.

oscars.note: Won 3 Oscars incl. Best Picture and Best Adapted Screenplay

adaptations.author: Thomas Keneally
adaptations.hook: Keneally's Booker Prize-winning account is as shattering as Spielberg's film — and more personal.

oscars.note: Won 7 Oscars incl. Best Picture and Best Director

adaptations.author: Winston Groom
adaptations.hook: The film is beloved; the novel is stranger, funnier, and far more unhinged — in the best way.

oscars.note: Won 6 Oscars incl. Best Picture and Best Director

adaptations.author: Chuck Palahniuk
adaptations.hook: Palahniuk's debut is leaner, darker, and ends differently — essential for anyone who loved the film.

adaptations.book_title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest
adaptations.author: Ken Kesey
adaptations.adaptation_title: One Flew Over the Cuckoo's Nest (1975)
adaptations.hook: Kesey wrote this while working night shifts at a psych ward — the novel's perspective cuts even deeper than the film.

oscars.note: Won 5 Oscars incl. Best Picture, Director, Actor, Actress, and Screenplay

adaptations.author: Thomas Harris
adaptations.hook: Harris gave us Lecter — the novel is as tightly wound as the film and harder to put down.

oscars.note: Won 5 Oscars incl. Best Picture, Director, Actor, Actress, and Screenplay

adaptations.author: Stephen King
adaptations.hook: King published this as a serial in six parts — still one of his most emotionally devastating works.

adaptations.author: Christopher Priest
adaptations.hook: Priest's novel layers the rivalry differently — two unreliable narrators, two magicians, one impossible trick.

oscars.note: Nominated: Best Cinematography and Best Art Direction

adaptations.author: Stephen King
adaptations.hook: Kubrick's film is a masterpiece — King's novel is a different kind of horror: more intimate, more heartbreaking.

adaptations.author: Michael Dobbs
adaptations.hook: Dobbs wrote the original UK novel — cynical, sharp, and every bit as Machiavellian as the show.

adaptations.author: Piper Kerman
adaptations.hook: Kerman's memoir is wittier and more sobering than the series — the real story behind the orange jumpsuit.

adaptations.author: Caleb Carr
adaptations.hook: Carr's Victorian crime novel is richly atmospheric — the show barely scratches its surface.

adaptations.author: Dan Simmons
adaptations.hook: Simmons blends Arctic survival horror with the supernatural — the novel is longer, stranger, and more terrifying.

adaptations.author: Petra Hammesfahr
adaptations.hook: Hammesfahr's psychological thriller is even more unsettling than the show — driven entirely from Judith's perspective.

adaptations.author: John Douglas
adaptations.hook: Douglas profiled real serial killers for the FBI — this is the memoir the show is built on.

adaptations.author: Margaret Atwood
adaptations.hook: Atwood's most unsettling novel — a true historical murder case told through unreliable memory.

adaptations.author: Jeff Lindsay
adaptations.hook: Lindsay's original novel is darker and funnier than the show's early seasons — where the real Dexter lives.

adaptations.author: Thomas Harris
adaptations.hook: Thomas Harris introduced Lecter in Red Dragon — as a supporting character, he's somehow even more chilling.

adaptations.author: Stephen Ambrose
adaptations.hook: Ambrose's military history reads like a novel — this is where Spielberg and Hanks started.
